## The 60-30-10 Rule

**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V-SD_zV9S2c
**Duration:** 2:29 | **Views:** 119K

### Transcript Preview

how do you use a color palette is there a rule as to how much of each color to use there is a rule in fact but rules are also meant to be broken but let's start with the rule so here I'll show you 60 30 10 they equal 100% so essentially this is kind of like the baseline color ratio that I would probably start with so the 60 percent is obviously the color that will be used the most 60 would be like your primary color so this is like your hero the 30% is your secondary color it's used about half as much but you know it's still fairly visible and then the 10% that's the accent color that's that's kind of like your little wild card where you see this work really well is in web design so maybe your background could be the secondary color if you want it light right this kind of life right here and then maybe your headers are blue or something right and then where you might use your accent color this little 10% is maybe in calls to action so like on a button or on something that pops up like things that you really want to get people's attention and you want them to look at that's where you would use that that little 10% yeah accent color a lot of times you'll have kind of different types of the same color and I like to keep...

## How to Not Suck at Color — 5 Tips

**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=C1rQQ_YpgcI
**Duration:** 7:52 | **Views:** 1.5M

### Transcript Preview

why is working with color so hard it's part art part science and if you don't know what you're doing mostly just frustrating hello friend i'm greg gunn and i'm here to help you make better color choices in this video i'll share five tips to keep in mind for whenever you find yourself struggling with a color wheel these are fundamental concepts that every artist and designer should know and understand and each one of them will help your work look more dynamic and cohesive that being said if you do want to learn color theory and go deeper about how to use it in your own work then check out my course color for creatives we cover everything you'd ever want to know about color theory as for the rest of you here comes tip number one color harmonies people love to ask hey greg where do i even start with color it's a good question and my answer to them is always start with color harmonies color harmonies are historically proven science-backed techniques to pair colors together in visually pleasing ways which sounds way more complicated than it actually is in fact you probably already know some color harmony terms like monochromatic complementary and analogous all they do is describe the relationship between where these colors are on the color wheel some apps even have color harmony tools built right into them like adobe illustrator's color guide and procreate's color harmony tool okay let's say you love...
